title: Docker Buildx CI, Tests, Context, Cache, and Multi-Platform Output · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Build Record Summary Debug
summary: A CI build sends the wrong Git context, ignores a generated file, leaks a credential through build args, trusts a warm cache, skips container behavior tests, and pushes before validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci build record summary debug increment in container build and test workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Retain build record, summary, stages, timings, cache use, inputs, warnings, digest, and controlled reproduction instructions without secrets.
Build declared platforms with Buildx, distinguish native and emulated execution, test each output, and verify the manifest index and platform digests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-build-record-summary-debug — Retain build record, summary, stages, timings, cache use, inputs, warnings, digest, and controlled reproduction instructions without secrets.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-docker-build-test-cache-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-docker-build-test-cache-3
    artifact: container-build-workflow.yaml
    decision: worked ci docker build test cache 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      setup-buildx-action: 4
      build-push-action: 7
      context: explicit-path-after-generation
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      platforms: [linux-amd64, linux-arm64]
      secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      cache-writer: trusted-main-only
      push: after-platform-tests
      build-record: retained
      output-digest: sha256-verified
    verify: render build inputs inspect build record test each platform changed config signals health and read-only behavior then verify index digest
    failure: Reject implicit Git context, ARG secrets, fork-writable cache, build-only proof, one-platform tests, or push-before-validation.
    repair: Select context deliberately, mount secrets, scope cache, test runtime behavior per platform, retain records, and push verified output.
